## Breaking Changes

- **`parse_*_ticks` / `parse_option_contracts_v3` / `parse_calendar_days_v3` now return `Result<Vec<T>, DecodeError>`** (#325, #326) -- the generated and hand-written row-decoders in `decode.rs` used to silently coalesce per-cell type mismatches to zero. Mismatches now propagate as `DecodeError::TypeMismatch { column, expected, observed }` and fold into `Error::Decode` at the `DirectClient` boundary. Rust callers that reached past `DirectClient::*` into the free functions need to handle the `Result`. SDK users hit `DirectClient` methods whose `Result<Vec<T>, Error>` return shape is unchanged; no FFI / Python / Go / C++ contract moves.
- **`Contract::option` returns `Result`** (#324) -- invalid `expiration` / `strike` / `right` now surface via `?` instead of panicking.
- **`tdbe` 0.10.0: FIT bulk decoder returns `FitRows`** (#324) -- replaces the previous `Vec<Vec<i32>>`. Access rows via `FitRows::row(i)` or `FitRows::iter()`. Internal allocator pressure on sustained FPSS streaming drops materially.
- **`Error::Decode` display text now reads "Decode failed: ..."** -- the variant now carries both protobuf deserialization errors and post-decode per-cell type-mismatch failures.

## Added

- **Per-request deadlines and async cancellation** (#298) -- every historical endpoint exposes `with_timeout_ms(u64)` or `with_deadline(Instant)` on the builder. Matching `WithTimeoutMs` / `WithDeadline` options land in Go, C FFI, Python, and C++ SDKs. Python surfaces a dedicated `TimeoutError` class distinct from `ThetaDataError` so slow-endpoint handling doesn't swallow other failures. Cancellation is cooperative via `tokio::time::timeout` on the gRPC future, so server-side work is freed promptly.
- **SDK surface fully declarative** (#300) -- every generated method signature, optional-parameter shape, streaming dispatch, FFI wrapper, Python binding, Go function, and C++ method is projected from `sdk_surface.toml`, `endpoint_surface.toml`, and `tick_schema.toml`. Adding or changing a method is a TOML edit plus `generate_sdk_surfaces`.
- **Full-vocabulary wildcard support for option contract parameters** (#284) -- `validate_expiration` accepts `*` / `YYYYMMDD` / `YYYY-MM-DD`. `validate_strike` accepts `*` / `0` / empty (wildcard) or a positive decimal. `direct::wire_strike_opt` and `direct::wire_right_opt` leave the proto field unset on wildcard inputs. A full option chain's open interest for QQQ now returns all 10,158 rows in ~1s (a single bulk call), down from a 34-expiration serial loop (~22s).
- **`tdbe::error::DecodeError` enum** (#325) -- per-cell decoding errors carry structured `{ column, expected, observed }` context.
- **`tdbe::codec::fit::FitRows`** (#324) -- typed container replacing `Vec<Vec<i32>>` from the bulk FIT decoder, with column-major access and reused backing buffers.
- **Live parameter-mode matrix validator** (#287, #288, #290, #291) -- every SDK release validator runs one cell per `(endpoint, mode)` pair instead of one per endpoint. Modes emitted by the endpoint generator from the wire shape; option `ContractSpec` endpoints get six modes each covering every wildcard cross-product. Per-optional-parameter `with_<param>` cells plus a compound `all_optionals` cell. Per-cell 60-second hard timeout. `scripts/validate_release.sh` runs CLI -> Python -> Go -> C++ -> agreement in order.
- **Cross-language agreement check** (#290, #291) -- `scripts/validate_agreement.py` asserts every `(endpoint, mode)` cell present in at least two SDKs agrees on `status` and `row_count`.
- **Upstream-derived tier and wildcard maps** (#290, #291) -- generator-time lookups against a pinned upstream OpenAPI snapshot, fail-closed on drift.
- **Structured field-level diff in validator output** (#293).
- **Public API redesign charter** (#282) -- `docs/public-api-redesign.md` lays out the layered ergonomic facade plan; the streaming category is named `realtime` rather than `live`.

## Changed

- **Major bloat cleanup** (#302) -- stripped roughly 1,500 lines of ceremony, over-abstraction, and redundant tests across `build_support/` and the SDK layers. Behavior identical, surface identical.
- **`fpss/mod.rs` split into focused submodules** (#327) -- 2,143-line file becomes `accumulator`, `decode`, `delta`, `events`, `io_loop`, `session`, and a slim `mod.rs`. Public behavior unchanged.
- **`build_support/endpoints.rs` split into a focused module tree** (#294) -- 2,700-line file becomes `helpers`, `model`, `modes`, `parser`, `render/*`.
- **Generator templates moved to `include_str!`** (#296, #301) -- every remaining `push_str(...)` emitter in `build_support` is now an `include_str!` of a `.tmpl` file under `build_support/endpoints/render/templates/`.
- **Test-mode fixtures now live in TOML** (#295) -- per-mode test-fixture values moved from a Rust match statement into `sdk_surface.toml`.
- **`scripts/check_tier_badges.py` live-fetches upstream `openapiv3.yaml`** (#280) -- removed `scripts/upstream_tiers.json` and pulls the authoritative `x-min-subscription` map at check time with 4 retries + exponential backoff.
- **Validator tier gating is server-driven** (#289, #290) -- dropped the static `VALIDATOR_ACCOUNT_TIER` env-var gate; the live server now classifies tier-restricted cells as SKIP. Wildcard-expiration modes are suppressed on the 7 endpoints bound to `expiration_no_star`.
- **gRPC `Status` code surfacing** (#303) -- server numeric error codes are now extracted from `Status` trailers as typed-map lookups, so failures like `INVALID_SYMBOL` read by name instead of as a raw integer.

## Fixed

All 13 correctness / security / perf findings from the public API audit (#324):

- FPSS client is now `Sync`-safe -- the internal read/write halves and session state are now properly guarded so sharing an `FpssClient` across threads is sound.
- Python streaming deadlock on shutdown -- `next_event()` releases the GIL before blocking on the ring buffer; shutdown coordinates with the blocking reader so Ctrl+C interrupts streaming cleanly.
- Python Ctrl+C interruptibility -- long-running gRPC calls release the GIL and cooperate with Python's signal handling.
- FFI `CString` interior-NUL swallowing -- string outputs now surface `CString::new` failures via `tdx_last_error` instead of silently truncating.
- Subscription drop on lock poison -- active FPSS subscriptions no longer vanish if a panic poisoned the state mutex.
- Protobuf `DataValue` type coercion -- mixed `Price` / `Number` encoding on OHLC cells is normalized consistently across all endpoints.
- Float -> i32 overflow and panic on invalid strike input -- strike parsing is now bounds-checked.
- Greeks recomputation memoized on the common `(spot, strike, vol, rate, t)` tuple so analytics endpoints no longer recompute identical Greeks on back-to-back rows.
- FIT decoder allocator thrash -- the bulk decoder reuses a single backing buffer through `FitRows`, cutting sustained streaming allocation rate by roughly an order of magnitude.
- Double string allocation on `Contract` clone -- `Contract` now wraps its symbol in `Arc<str>`.
- JSON serialization moved off the FPSS I/O thread -- `next_event` returns typed structs; JSON is only paid at the FFI boundary when callers ask for it.
- `parse_right` no longer panics on unrecognized input -- returns a structured error.
- `AuthResponse::session_token` redacted in `Debug` (see **Security**).

Also:

- **Go TLS error-channel races on reconnect** (#324) -- closing an FPSS TLS connection concurrently with an in-flight read no longer produces a send-on-closed-channel panic. CGo callbacks are pinned to the calling OS thread for TLS thread-local consistency.
- **FFI `CString` null handling** (#303) and **Go error-check unification** (#303).
- **Unset `DataValue` oneof fails loud in every strict decoder** (#326) -- the option contract and calendar day decoders now return `DecodeError::TypeMismatch { observed: "Unset" }` instead of coalescing to `0`. `NullValue` is still coalesced (legitimate null).
- **Option contract wildcard rejection** (#284) -- before this release the SDK had no working path to the server's bulk-chain mode: `*` was rejected client-side by `validate_expiration`, and `0` was rejected server-side.
- **Validator tier detection drift** (#289) -- legitimate server responses previously classified as SKIP are now attempted.
- **CI unbroken on `main`** (#299) -- fixed a `timeout_ms` TOML field mismatch and made the Go pin-test CRLF-robust.
- **FPSS internal visibility tightening** -- `active_subs` and `active_full_subs` are now `pub(in crate::fpss)` rather than `pub(super)`; reconnect-delay tests assert against the `TOO_MANY_REQUESTS_DELAY_MS` / `RECONNECT_DELAY_MS` constants instead of hard-coded millisecond literals.

## Security

- **Session token no longer leaks via `Debug`** (#324) -- `AuthResponse`'s `session_token` field is now redacted in its `Debug` impl. Previously a `tracing::debug!("{auth:?}")` call would write the bearer token into logs. Credentials were already redacted; this closes the parallel leak on the response side.

## Internal

- **Consolidated CI workflow cleanup** (#323) -- shared Rust-dep setup via a reusable composite action (`.github/actions/setup-rust-deps`), removed duplicated steps, narrowed `live` to manual dispatch.
- **Python abi3 smoke CI no longer rebuilds the wheel** (#304) -- reuses the wheel built earlier in the pipeline.
- **Per-cell rationale + redundancy audit** (#297) -- generated test cells carry a one-line rationale.

Full changelog: https://github.com/userFRM/ThetaDataDx/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
